purpose: Public evidence-badge and claim-classifier system for Matrix Reprogrammed. It tells readers what a record proves, what it does not prove, and what source would strengthen a claim.
rule: Source first. Evidence class second. Claim strength third. Network interpretation last.

badges.label: Court Record
badges.proves: A filing, order, docket, exhibit, testimony transcript, judgment, plea, or court-managed document exists.
badges.doesNotProve: It does not automatically prove every allegation inside the filing unless the court found it, the party admitted it, or the record independently supports it.
badges.strengthensWith: Ruling, exhibit, sworn transcript, plea, verdict, authenticated docket entry.

badges.label: Official Source
badges.proves: A government, regulator, court, agency, committee, or official dataset published the material.
badges.doesNotProve: It may still need context, date, definitions, limitations, redactions, and category separation.
badges.strengthensWith: Original publication page, dataset notes, definitions, release memo, archive capture.

badges.label: Declassified Archive
badges.proves: A released file, FOIA record, archive item, or historical government document exists.
badges.doesNotProve: It does not automatically prove present-day claims or the full surrounding context.
badges.strengthensWith: File date, agency origin, release package, related files, independent corroboration.

badges.label: Email / Message Record
badges.proves: A correspondence record, message thread, sender/recipient/date/context trail, or archive result exists.
badges.doesNotProve: It does not automatically prove the truth of statements made inside the message.
badges.strengthensWith: Authenticated archive, full thread, metadata, attachments, matching public records.

badges.label: Flight / Log / Contact Record
badges.proves: A name, trip, contact, phone-book entry, schedule, log, or proximity record exists.
badges.doesNotProve: It does not prove criminal conduct, knowledge, intent, or participation by itself.
badges.strengthensWith: Multiple records, testimony, date alignment, corroborating emails, court exhibits.

badges.label: Sworn Claim
badges.proves: A person made a claim under oath or in a legal setting.
badges.doesNotProve: A sworn claim is not the same as a court finding or conviction.
badges.strengthensWith: Corroborating documents, cross-examination, court acceptance, supporting exhibits.

badges.label: Settlement / NDA
badges.proves: A private legal resolution, silence agreement, or civil compromise exists or is credibly reported.
badges.doesNotProve: It is not an admission unless the settlement says it is.
badges.strengthensWith: Settlement text, court filings, party statements, payment terms, related testimony.

badges.label: Financial / Access Record
badges.proves: Money, payments, donations, employment, advisory access, institutional access, or financial proximity exists.
badges.doesNotProve: Money or access is not automatic proof of sex-crime participation or operational knowledge.
badges.strengthensWith: Invoices, bank records, filings, internal reviews, testimony, contemporaneous correspondence.

badges.label: Credible Reporting / News Lead
badges.proves: A reputable outlet has reported a claim, source trail, legal update, or document lead.
badges.doesNotProve: Reporting is a lead unless backed by primary documents, direct evidence, or court findings.
badges.strengthensWith: Primary records, multiple independent outlets, linked documents, named on-record sources.

badges.label: Interpretive / Symbolic Claim
badges.proves: An interpretation, pattern, symbolic reading, or analytical argument has been made.
badges.doesNotProve: It does not prove criminal conduct or conspiracy by itself.
badges.strengthensWith: Primary text, historical source, doctrinal source, direct admission, external corroboration.

badges.label: Speculation Quarantine
badges.proves: A hypothesis exists but does not yet have enough evidence to publish as a claim.
badges.doesNotProve: It proves nothing beyond a research lead.
badges.strengthensWith: Primary records, named sources, court documents, official releases, authenticated archives.

badges.label: Unsupported / Rejected
badges.proves: The claim lacks sufficient sourcing or violates the site evidence boundary.
badges.doesNotProve: It should not be promoted as fact.
badges.strengthensWith: A real public source trail before publication.

claimRules: A name in a record is not proof of wrongdoing.
A symbol is not proof of conspiracy.
An adverse-event report is not proof of causation.
A relationship line must say what it means.
Unsupported viral claims are quarantined or rejected.
